catalyst: Desktop Node public rollout with 2x points multiplier
evidence: Official @grass post said Desktop Node became available to everyone after invite-only beta; earlier thread said it uses <5% of Chromium resources, handles 10x more bandwidth than the extension, and gets a 2x network-score multiplier.
direct_impact: Improves supply quality and user reward potential by shifting users from browser extension to a native node.
second_order: Higher-quality uptime/bandwidth strengthens Grass' AI data supply story and makes points/reward design more defensible.
watch_next: Track Desktop Node multiplier changes, node quality metrics, active node count, uptime/bandwidth, and anti-Sybil enforcement.

catalyst: GRASS network/token launch and Airdrop One
evidence: @grassfdn announced GRASS Airdrop One for Oct. 28, 2024 at 13:30 UTC, with eligibility requiring 500+ Grass Points in any epoch and a Solana wallet linked by Oct. 14, 2024 20:00 UTC.
direct_impact: Creates the liquid token/reward layer for the network and converts pre-token points participation into a token-holder base.
second_order: Sets precedent for future seasons/claims, staking, governance, and reward expectations among node operators.
watch_next: Watch future airdrop/season mechanics, claim extensions, eligibility rules, token utility, and scam-link warnings.

catalyst: Grass Staking Program live / delegation to routers
evidence: Official Grass Foundation staking page/terms state token holders may delegate GRASS to Grass Routers or approved third-party routers and earn staking rewards; terms show Last Revised: February 20, 2025.
direct_impact: Introduces a token sink and reward mechanism tied to routers / network participation.
second_order: Staking participation can reduce liquid supply and creates a token-holder incentive layer around network infrastructure.
watch_next: Track total GRASS staked, APR/reward changes, router list, third-party router approvals, unbonding rules, and any security incidents.

catalyst: Android app launch with temporary 3x Grass Points
evidence: Official Grass blog and @grass launch thread said Grass on Android is live via direct download and Google Play, runs in the background, auto-pauses on low battery/poor connection, and Android users earn 3x Grass Points for a limited time.
direct_impact: Expands node supply beyond desktop/browser users and lowers global participation friction.
second_order: Mobile supply can improve geographic coverage and uptime density; later updates said mobile reached a material share of network activity.
watch_next: Watch Android retention, Play Store availability, mobile share of network activity, multiplier sunset, battery/privacy complaints, and future OS support.

catalyst: Network Points launch / quality-weighted reward system
evidence: @0xdrej said Network Points launched as a major upgrade that compensates participants based on actual economic value created by shared bandwidth / residential IP, while bad actors spoofing participation "won’t like" Airdrop Two.
direct_impact: Changes rewards from simple uptime farming toward data-quality and economic-value contribution.
second_order: Makes future airdrop/reward distribution more defensible and could reduce low-quality/Sybil supply in the network.
watch_next: Watch Airdrop Two criteria, Network Points dashboards, anti-Sybil enforcement, wallet rollout, and whether real contributors see materially better rewards.

evidence: Official @grass post stated enterprise revenue flows directly to the Grass Foundation, Wynd Labs is a service provider executing the network mandate, and this structure is intended to make value accrue to the ecosystem.
direct_impact: Addresses the core token-value-accrual question: who captures enterprise AI/data revenue.
second_order: If verifiable revenue reaches the Foundation, treasury management, staking rewards, buybacks, and ecosystem incentives become more credible.
watch_next: Watch for audited/transparent revenue disclosures, foundation wallet activity, service-provider fee disclosures, and legal/governance updates.

evidence: Official @grass Treasury Update said Grass completed $250,000 in open-market GRASS purchases after an earlier $100,000 allocation completed in November; post included a treasury wallet / proof-of-reserves address.
direct_impact: Concrete value-accrual / treasury-management action using open-market token purchases.
second_order: Repeated purchases could support token-holder confidence, but impact depends on cadence, size, and transparency versus circulating liquidity.
watch_next: Track treasury wallet, future purchase announcements, purchase source funding, whether this becomes recurring policy, and whether buybacks connect to revenue.

catalyst: Quarterly data-sales revenue scaled to 8 figures
evidence: @0xdrej said Grass has strong real revenue from data sales to frontier AI labs, with quarterly revenue scaled to 8 figures and doubling quarter-over-quarter for the prior year.
direct_impact: Provides a concrete revenue-scale datapoint for the AI data network thesis.
second_order: If revenue continues flowing to the Foundation, future treasury actions, staking rewards, or ecosystem incentives become more credible.
watch_next: Watch audited revenue disclosures, customer concentration, Foundation wallet flows, treasury purchases, and July 7 call follow-through.

evidence: Official @grass post said GRASS is live on OKX spot and the GRASS/USDT pair is available.
direct_impact: Improves centralized exchange liquidity and access for GRASS.
second_order: Large-exchange liquidity can broaden the holder base and make treasury/staking/reward flows more investable, but also raises sensitivity to unlocks and sell pressure.
watch_next: Track OKX volume/depth, subsequent major exchange listings, funding/derivatives listings, and token unlock/liquidity events.

catalyst: Next GRASS Token Holder and Network Participant Call / roadmap update
evidence: Official @grass post said the next GRASS Token Holder and Network Participant Call will take place July 7 and cover network progress, development roadmap, business milestones, growth highlights, and future ecosystem plans.
direct_impact: Near-term official venue for roadmap, business milestones, and token/network updates.
second_order: Could clarify rewards/season mechanics, revenue, staking, mobile growth, and ecosystem direction; disappointment risk if it only repeats high-level updates.
watch_next: Watch exact time/registration, agenda, transcript/recording, Season 2 or reward details, roadmap commitments, and business/customer metrics.

catalyst: 2026 expansion from training data to inference data
timing: 2026 focus; no specific delivery date announced
catalyst_date_label: 2026 focus; no specific delivery date announced; date needs confirmation
evidence: @0xdrej 2025 recap said Grass broke 3PB/day scraped data, scaled quarterly data-sales revenue to 8 figures, and that a big 2026 focus is expanding from training to inference data.
direct_impact: Inference data would expand Grass beyond training-data collection into a potentially larger, more real-time AI demand category.
second_order: Success could strengthen enterprise revenue and token-value-accrual narrative; failure/delay would leave the catalyst as founder-level roadmap language only.
watch_next: Watch official product docs, customer announcements, inference-data revenue, data-quality benchmarks, and roadmap confirmation on the July 7 call.

catalyst: New Orb and World ID 3.0 unveiled
evidence: Official World launch posts unveiled the next-generation Orb and World ID 3.0 with new credentials, privacy/security/scalability upgrades, and deepfake-prevention features.
direct_impact: Upgrades the core proof-of-human hardware/protocol stack and lays out scaling toward much larger verification coverage.
second_order: Better Orb throughput and stronger credentials/privacy can reduce verification bottlenecks and expand use cases beyond crypto.
watch_next: Watch Orb deployment pace, country availability, privacy/regulatory reviews, and World ID upgrade adoption.

catalyst: World Chain mainnet opened to World users
evidence: Official World blog said World Chain is live and that all 15M World ID holders and World App users had either migrated or were in the process of migrating to World Chain.
direct_impact: Launches World’s own human-prioritized L2 as the settlement layer for World App, World ID, Mini Apps, and ecosystem activity.
second_order: Creates the base for gas sponsorship, native apps, stablecoins, and verified-human distribution as onchain infrastructure rather than only an identity app.
watch_next: Track World Chain transactions, active verified users, Mini App usage, bridge/stablecoin liquidity, and developer adoption.

catalyst: Mini Apps early scale and incubator launch
evidence: Official World blog said Mini Apps reached as many as 5.4M opens per day from more than 1M unique humans and announced new social/developer features plus an incubator.
direct_impact: Shows World App becoming a developer distribution surface rather than only an identity wallet.
second_order: Mini App scale gives builders a reason to integrate World ID / World Chain and can drive repeat World App usage.
watch_next: Track Mini App DAU/retention, developer submissions, top apps, transaction volume, and grant/incubator outcomes.

catalyst: World launches in the United States
evidence: Official World blog announced that World launched in the USA.
direct_impact: Expands World into the most important AI/tech consumer market and a critical regulatory jurisdiction.
second_order: US availability can accelerate partners, developers, and mainstream proof-of-humanity use cases, but also increases regulatory/privacy scrutiny.
watch_next: Track US Orb locations, adoption, state-level availability, regulatory reactions, and US partner integrations.

catalyst: Native USDC and CCTP V2 live on World Chain
evidence: Official World blog said Circle USDC and CCTP V2 are live on World Chain and that existing bridged USDC was converted to native USDC.
direct_impact: Adds core stablecoin and cross-chain transfer infrastructure to World Chain.
second_order: Native USDC improves payments, remittances, Mini App commerce, and developer viability inside World App / World Chain.
watch_next: Track USDC liquidity, payment volume, bridge flows, Mini App integrations, and stablecoin UX in World App.

catalyst: $135M WLD purchase by a16z and Bain Capital Crypto
evidence: Official World blog said World raised $135M from Andreessen Horowitz and Bain Capital Crypto through a WLD purchase to fund network expansion.
direct_impact: Material funding and token-demand event to support Orb/network expansion.
second_order: Institutional support strengthens runway and expansion credibility, but market impact depends on token terms, unlocks, and treasury flows.
watch_next: Watch use of proceeds, Orb deployment acceleration, investor/token lockups, wallet movements, and future financing.

catalyst: Developer Rewards increased to $100K/week; ecosystem surpassed 500 Mini Apps
evidence: Official World blog said the Dev Rewards pilot increased to $100K per week as the ecosystem surpassed 500 Mini Apps, more than 2M daily opens, and over 100M WLD transacted in Mini Apps.
direct_impact: Expands developer incentives while reporting meaningful ecosystem usage metrics.
second_order: Recurring rewards plus 500+ Mini Apps can compound World App utility if retention and monetization improve.
watch_next: Track weekly rewards, top Mini App retention, WLD transaction quality, developer churn, and whether rewards continue or convert to organic growth.

catalyst: Polymarket Mini App launched in World App
evidence: Official World blog said the Polymarket Mini App became accessible inside World App, with users able to participate using WLD or USDC.
direct_impact: Adds a high-profile consumer/financial Mini App and direct WLD/USDC utility inside World App.
second_order: Prediction-market integration can improve World App engagement and demonstrate distribution value for major apps.
watch_next: Track Polymarket Mini App users, volume, regional availability, compliance constraints, and repeat financial-app integrations.

catalyst: New World App with World Chat, payments, and expanded Mini Apps
evidence: Tools for Humanity official X announced the new World App with secure World Chat, global payments, and expanded Mini Apps inside the World ecosystem.
direct_impact: Turns World App from identity wallet into a consumer app surface for verified-human chat, payments, and mini-app usage.
second_order: More daily app utility can support verified-human distribution for builders and make World ID less dependent on one-off signup incentives.
watch_next: Track World App MAU, Mini App retention, payments volume, verified-user activity, and whether chat/payments drive repeat usage.

evidence: Official @world_chain_ post announced World Build 3 with up to $200K grants, distribution to 38M+ users, mentorship, funded Seoul/San Francisco trips, and support for founders building human-verified apps.
direct_impact: Bootstraps the World Chain/Mini Apps developer funnel with grant capital and distribution.
second_order: A strong cohort can create proof that World's verified-human user base is useful to third-party builders, not only internal apps.
watch_next: Track winners, shipped apps, grant disbursements, active users, retention, and whether apps use World ID/World Chain in a differentiated way.

catalyst: AgentKit beta / Proof of Human for agentic automation
evidence: Official @worldnetwork post launched AgentKit beta as the human layer for agentic automation, letting AI-agent workflows prove they are controlled by a real human through World ID.
direct_impact: Extends World ID from login/identity into AI-agent permissioning and bot-resistant automation.
second_order: If adopted by ticketing, commerce, SaaS, or agent platforms, World can become identity middleware for the agentic web rather than only a crypto app.
watch_next: Watch AgentKit docs, integrations, developer usage, Okta/enterprise links, and real deployments beyond demos.

evidence: Official World blog said MiniKit 2.0 is live on World Chain, enabling builders to build once and deploy across web and World App with Flashblocks and gas sponsorship.
direct_impact: Improves developer UX for Mini Apps / World Chain deployments.
second_order: Lower build friction can increase developer throughput and make the World App distribution channel easier to target.
watch_next: Track MiniKit adoption, new app launches, developer retention, gas sponsorship usage, and app quality.

catalyst: Lift Off partner suite / new World ID integrations
evidence: Official Lift Off announcement described the most significant World ID protocol upgrade to date and partner integrations including Tinder, Concert Kit, Zoom, Docusign, Vercel, and Okta.
direct_impact: Moves World ID into consumer, enterprise, ticketing, and agentic-web workflows.
second_order: A broad partner suite validates proof-of-humanity demand beyond crypto, but needs real usage metrics and repeat deployments.
watch_next: Track rollout status for each partner, usage metrics, enterprise adoption, privacy/regulatory feedback, and follow-on integrations.

catalyst: World + Tinder humans-only / verified dating integration
evidence: Official @worldnetwork Lift Off / launch-day post said the World + Tinder integration went live in the US, with World ID verification used for humans-only experiences and perks.
direct_impact: Shows World ID moving into a mainstream consumer app vertical where bot/fake-account resistance is a clear pain point.
second_order: High-profile consumer integrations can validate proof-of-humanity demand outside crypto and create follow-on partnership leverage.
watch_next: Track geographic rollout, user uptake, Tinder/Match disclosures, privacy messaging, and additional dating/social app integrations.

catalyst: 40M World App users / 18M Orb-verified humans milestone
evidence: Official @worldnetwork post reported 40M World App users and 18M Orb-verified humans across 160 countries by April 2026.
direct_impact: Confirms scale of the verified-human distribution base that World sells to builders and partners.
second_order: Large verified-user numbers make Mini Apps, AgentKit, ticketing, and consumer integrations more credible, but quality depends on activity/retention not only signups.
watch_next: Track monthly verified-user growth, active World App users, Orb deployment geography, retention, Mini App usage, and regulatory pauses.

catalyst: World ID / AgentKit for bot-resistant ticketing
evidence: World Network official posts announced/boosted Thirty Seconds to Mars tour ticketing with World ID verification for humans-only tickets and highlighted Agent Kit as anti-bot ticketing infrastructure.
direct_impact: Concrete real-world use case where World ID blocks bots and gives verified humans access/perks.
second_order: Ticketing is a high-signal vertical for proof-of-humanity; repeat artist/venue partnerships could turn World ID into anti-scalper infrastructure.
watch_next: Watch redemption/attendance metrics, partner repeats, ticketing platform integrations, and whether World shares fraud-reduction data.

catalyst: WLD unlock rate decreases by 43% starting July 2026
catalyst_date_label: July 2026
evidence: World Foundation official X and blog said the WLD unlock rate will decrease by 43% in July 2026; funds continue supporting operations, R&D, Orb manufacturing, and ecosystem growth.
direct_impact: Directly changes token supply-pressure schedule and is material to WLD market structure.
second_order: Lower unlock rate can reduce incremental sell pressure, but market response depends on circulating supply, actual wallets, demand, and whether OTC/treasury sales continue.
watch_next: Watch July unlock execution, Foundation/TFH wallet flows, unlock dashboards, OTC sales, and circulating-supply updates.

catalyst: HYPE options market share / Derive as primary HYPE options venue
evidence: CEO replies during the 2026-06-15 scan said Derive is the primary on/offchain HYPE options venue and supports stable/HYPE/kHYPE margin plus portfolio margining: https://x.com/nickforster/status/2066662428814049501 and https://x.com/nickforster/status/2066623266534605136
direct_impact: More listed-underlying demand and HYPE-native collateral can drive OI, fees, and DRV buyback capacity.
